# Transcrição: 01- Course 4 - Selecting Your Market & Investment Criteria

## [00:00 - 03:05]

Selecting your market and investment criteria. To be successful as a real estate investor, you need to know your market. You have to know your market. You must know the market. This isn't just some passive understanding of the local economy or locations or good parts or bad parts of neighborhood. You have to know everything there is to know about the market that you're in. Are you in a buyer or seller's market? Is this a good growing part of your city or is this a dying part of the city? You have to dig in and understand what makes your market tick and from there what constitutes a good deal for you in a particular neighborhood, for your business that you're building and for the bottom line and future appreciation that you expect to create wealth with the real estate you're investing in. Look it sounds simple enough that you know your market but too often people gloss over this critical step. You must know everything about the market. Rent comps, vacancy comps, comparables, comps means comparables, occupancy comparables, the A, the B, the C properties in that market. What's coming to market, the jobs in that market? Look when that happens, those that gloss over this step, these would be real estate investors wind up either jumping at the first thing that they run into or they can never lock down on a deal. They literally never can make a move. Why? Because they don't know the market. You have to know the market better than the lender, the broker and the seller. Both of these ends of the spectrum. I don't move or I move too quickly or problematic and both will only serve to push you further away from your long term goals to create wealth using real estate. Look that's not you. You're a smart, savvy, strategic and more importantly an all-in investor which means you have to know your market. You need to know everything about that market, the price per unit, the average rehab costs in that area of town. One side of town may cost more money to rehab than another. One part of town may not even warrant, won't be able to afford the rehab. So you don't want to spend money where that part of your town cannot even afford it and they won't pay the rents necessary to warrant you doing such. You have to know the GRM there. You have to know what the NOI is there. You have to know the cap rates. You have to know whether the lenders are even willing to lend money in that particular marketplace. Look, you've got to get all in on this thing. You have to become a smart, savvy, strategic all in and know everything about your market real estate investor. Peel back the layers and dive into the market so you can start generating long-term wealth from apartment building and investments. That means what? You have to know everything. Take all the terminology that I gave you in an earlier course, the GRM, the NOI, the

## [03:05 - 04:27]

debt, the equity necessary, the cap X in a market, the expenses in a market, utilities, the insurance, all those terms that I gave you and apply it to your local market because different parts of a market can have different insurance. You might be in one part of the market in Houston, Texas, where you are required to have or carry flood insurance and another area where you aren't required. You might have a building that has a history of flooding but another one that doesn't. Just because a property has to have flood insurance doesn't mean it's had flood history. That's what I mean by you need to know everything. What rents can a market warrant? What cap X expenditures can your property warrant and that the tenants will pay for? You don't want to redo your floors and your kitchen if your tenant can't pay for it. You need to get yourself in a situation where you can spot a great deal from a mile away and have total certainty to pull the trigger. Look, I can look at properties on a Google search and know immediately whether I'm buying it or not. You can look at properties in different markets and know what the tenant looks like, what the rent looks like, what the price per unit looks like. That's where we have to get you so that you'll know exactly when a good deal appears. You can pull the trigger, line up your investors, line up your money, line up your debt and move forward.
